metadata.author: Paul
metadata.subject: Christ Replacing the Law and Being Formed in the Believers.

2: I am crucified with Christ; and it is no longer I who live, but it is Christ who lives in me; and the life which I now live in the flesh I live in faith, the faith of the Son of God, who loved me and gave Himself up for me.
3: Christ has redeemed us out of the curse of the law, having become a curse on our behalf; because it is written, 'Cursed is every one hanged on a tree'.
5: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, long-suffering, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, meekness, self-control; against such things there is no law.

20.text: The Christian life is not a matter of self-improvement, but of Christ living in the believer as their new life and person.

22.text: Fruit is the outward expression of the inner life of the Spirit. It is a single 'fruit' with many characteristics, produced by the organic growth of the Spirit in us.
